title: Ranunculus tinctisepalus
taxonomy: Ranunculus tinctisepalus is actually known as Ranunculus alpestris, a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It belongs to the Ranunculus genus, which comprises over 600 species of plants. The genus Ranunculus is part of the larger order Ranunculales, which includes many other flowering plant families. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus alpestris is a part of a diverse group of plants commonly known as buttercups.
